methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
CORE POWER is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Mikal Bøe that has raised $100M+. The valuation exercise draws on a broader Climate & Energy peer set of 9 companies (1 public, 8 private), including adjacent-stage names such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or narrow-tier comparables—only one adjacent-stage peer exists in the filtered dataset, falling short of the three-company threshold required for robust triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($100M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Growth-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied valuation of $120M. This method reflects the company's advancement through its funding lifecycle and assumes standard progression economics within the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. The range reflects downside and upside variance around the single applicable method rather than cross-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-fit public or private comparables at identical stage and scale, which limits precision. The valuation is fundamentally anchored to capital raised and stage mechanics rather than market-based peer pricing, warranting caution if transaction data or near-peer valuations emerge.
